title: feat(gen): serve OpenAPI spec at /openapi.json
author: voodoohop
pr_body_excerpt:
- The OpenAPI spec already existed (served at `/docs/open-api/generate-schema`); this just exposes it at the conventional `/openapi.json` path
- Extracts `buildMergedOpenApiSpec` so both routes share one source of truth (no logic duplication)
- Keeps `/openapi.json` indexable (excluded from the noindex middleware)
- Test asserts JSON output + parity with the canonical route

Pure routing change. Tests 8/8 green; reviewed by adversarial workflow + Codex (both APPROVE).

Addresses #11894

gist.headline: The map is on the table
gist.blurb: We've exposed our OpenAPI spec at the standard /openapi.json path. Now your favorite API tools can auto-discover our endpoints without breaking a sweat.
gist.summary: Exposed the existing OpenAPI specification at the conventional /openapi.json endpoint by extracting and sharing the schema generation logic.
gist.impact: Developers and API tools can now automatically discover and consume the pollinations.ai API schema using standard tooling.
gist.visual_concept: Polly the bee holding a blueprint or map (representing the OpenAPI spec) and showing it to the monitor robot, with a signpost pointing to the new standard path.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot holding a glowing blue blueprint map, showing it to a chunky pixel robot with a CRT monitor head. A wooden signpost nearby reads '/openapi.json' in pixel font. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and lavender gradient background. Warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley. Emotionally warm, nostalgic but beautiful.
